title: teucrium murcicum subsp. hieronymi
intro: Teucrium murcicum subsp. hieronymi, also known as Hieronymus's Teucrium, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family Lamiaceae. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which help to soothe respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The medicinal properties of Hieronymus's Teucrium are attributed to its rich content of essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which provide numerous health benefits, including antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-cancer properties. Herbal preparations of Hieronymus's Teucrium can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and are often used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory problems, digestive issues, and skin conditions.
